references.passiveText: There's an old oath the veterans used to swear. Most people have forgotten it, but some still live by it.
references.triggerDialogue: {target_npc} once told me the words: 'The post does not abandon the people, even when the people abandon the post.' I thought it was just ceremony. Now I see there are still those who mean every word. If you pass through {location}, you might find others who remember what that oath demands.

references.passiveText: They say someone once held the mountain pass alone for three days. I wonder if that story is about to repeat itself.
references.triggerDialogue: It happened a generation ago, near {location}. A single defender held a narrow pass against a raiding force while an entire village fled to safety. {target_npc} says they found the defender's journal years later: no regret, no fear, just a quiet accounting of hours held and lives saved. Some people are built different, I suppose.
